scheme_name: Post Matric Scholarship for SC Students
description: Financial assistance for SC category students pursuing post-matric education. Covers maintenance allowance, reimbursement of compulsory non-refundable fees, and study tour charges. Available for students enrolled in recognized institutions across India.

scheme_name: Pre Matric Scholarship for OBC Students
description: Scholarship for OBC students studying in Class 1 to 10. Family income must not exceed ₹2.5 lakh per annum. Provides admission fee, tuition fee, and maintenance allowance for day scholars and hostellers.

scheme_name: Central Sector Scheme of Scholarships for College and University Students
description: Scholarship for students from families with annual income less than ₹8 lakh. Provides ₹10,000 per annum for graduation and ₹20,000 per annum for post-graduation. Based on merit in Class 12 board exams.
AICTE scholarship for girl students admitted to first year of degree or diploma in AICTE-approved institutions. Provides ₹50,000 per annum including ₹2,000 per month as incidental charges.
Direct income support of ₹6,000 per year to farmer families across India, paid in three equal instalments. Aimed at supplementing financial needs of small and marginal farmers.

scheme_name: PM SVANidhi — Street Vendor Loan Scheme
description: Micro-credit of up to ₹50,000 for street vendors affected by COVID-19 to restart their livelihoods. Collateral-free working capital loan with interest subsidy.
Housing subsidy scheme for urban poor. Provides credit-linked subsidy of up to ₹2.67 lakh for purchase, construction, or enhancement of houses.

scheme_name: Ayushman Bharat — PM Jan Arogya Yojana
description: Health cover of ₹5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary hospitalization. Covers over 1,500 medical procedures. Largest government-funded health protection scheme in the world.
Small savings scheme for a girl child below 10 years. Parents can open an account with a minimum deposit of ₹250. Offers high interest rate (currently 8.2%) with tax benefits under Section 80C.
Financial assistance of ₹51,000 to families of SC girls at the time of marriage. Applicable for Punjab domicile families with annual income below ₹3 lakh.
Government reimburses 25% of prescribed stipend to employers who engage apprentices. Provides on-the-job training for youth. Applicable for candidates who have completed Class 5 or higher.
Bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 crore to SC/ST and women entrepreneurs for setting up a greenfield enterprise. At least one SC/ST borrower and one woman borrower per bank branch.
Loans up to ₹10 lakh for non-corporate, non-farm small and micro enterprises. Three categories: Shishu (up to ₹50K), Kishore (₹50K–5L), Tarun (₹5L–10L). No collateral required.

scheme_name: National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP) — Old Age Pension
description: Monthly pension of ₹200–₹500 for destitute elderly persons aged 60 and above who are below the poverty line. Central government contribution, states may add top-up.
